tool.name: Map with minibwa
tool.description: fast short and accurate long-read aligner, the successor of bwa-mem
tool.command:
@pipefail@
## Symlink the reference and build the minibwa index (sets $ref_index) @set_reference@
## Assemble read group information @define_read_group_helpers@ #if str($fastq_input.fastq_input_selector) == "paired": #set $rg_auto_name = $read_group_name_default($fastq_input.fastq_input1, $fastq_input.fastq_input2) #elif str($fastq_input.fastq_input_selector) == "paired_collection": #set $rg_auto_name = $read_group_name_default($fastq_input.fastq_input1.forward, $fastq_input.fastq_input1.reverse) #else: #set $rg_auto_name = $read_group_name_default($fastq_input.fastq_input1) #end if @set_use_rg_var@ @set_read_group_vars@
minibwa map -t "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8}" -x '$mapping.preset'
## Mapping options #if str($mapping.k): -k $mapping.k #end if #if str($mapping.c): -c $mapping.c #end if #if str($mapping.g): -g $mapping.g #end if #if str($mapping.w): -w $mapping.w #end if #if str($mapping.W): -W $mapping.W #end if #if str($mapping.m): -m $mapping.m #end if #if str($mapping.p): -p $mapping.p #end if #if str($mapping.N): -N $mapping.N #end if $mapping.chain_only
## Alignment options #if str($alignment.A): -A $alignment.A #end if #if str($alignment.B): -B $alignment.B #end if #if str($alignment.O).strip(): -O '$alignment.O' #end if #if str($alignment.E).strip(): -E '$alignment.E' #end if #if str($alignment.s): -s $alignment.s #end if
## Paired-end options #if str($fastq_input.fastq_input_selector) in ("paired", "paired_collection"): $fastq_input.P #if str($fastq_input.rescue): --rescue=$fastq_input.rescue #end if #end if
## Common / input-output options #if str($io_options.l_short): -l $io_options.l_short #end if #if str($io_options.base_tag) != "none": -b $io_options.base_tag #end if $io_options.hic $io_options.u $io_options.y $io_options.Y $io_options.five #if str($io_options.outn): --outn=$io_options.outn #end if #if str($io_options.xa): --xa=$io_options.xa #end if
#if $use_rg: @set_rg_string@ -R '$rg_string' #end if
#if str($output_format) == "paf": -f #end if
$ref_index #if str($fastq_input.fastq_input_selector) == "paired": '${fastq_input.fastq_input1}' '${fastq_input.fastq_input2}' #elif str($fastq_input.fastq_input_selector) == "paired_collection": '${fastq_input.fastq_input1.forward}' '${fastq_input.fastq_input1.reverse}' #else: '${fastq_input.fastq_input1}' #end if
#if str($output_format) == "bam": | samtools sort -@\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2} -T "\${TMPDIR:-.}" -O bam -o '$output' #else: > '$output' #end if

param.name: fastq_input_selector
param.label: Single or paired-end reads
param.help: Select between single-end, paired and paired collection data

param.name: fastq_input1
param.label: Select fastq dataset
param.help: Specify dataset with single-end or long reads

param.name: fastq_input1
param.label: Select first set of reads
param.help: Specify dataset with forward reads

param.name: fastq_input2
param.label: Select second set of reads
param.help: Specify dataset with reverse reads

param.label: Skip pairing and mate rescue
param.help: Map the two read sets independently, without read pairing or mate rescue

param.label: Mate rescue candidates
param.help: Mate rescue for up to this many candidates; set to 0 to skip rescue (default: 10)

param.name: fastq_input1
param.label: Select a paired collection
param.help: See help section for an explanation of dataset collections

param.label: Skip pairing and mate rescue
param.help: Map the two read sets independently, without read pairing or mate rescue

param.label: Mate rescue candidates
param.help: Mate rescue for up to this many candidates; set to 0 to skip rescue (default: 10)

param.name: output_format
param.label: Select an output format
param.help: BAM is a sorted, compressed alignment file. PAF is a compact tabular mapping format without base-level alignment.

param.name: preset
param.label: Preset
param.help: Choose a preset tuned to your read type. 'adap' auto-detects mixed short/long reads.

param.label: Minimum seed length
param.help: Default: 19

param.label: Maximum seed occurrences
param.help: Default: 250

param.label: Maximum gap size
param.help: Controls extension and chain breaking. Default: 100

param.label: Bandwidth
param.help: Default: 100

param.label: Long bandwidth
param.help: Used for long reads or the adaptive mode. Default: 20000

param.label: Minimum chaining score
param.help: Default: 25

param.label: Minimum secondary-to-primary score ratio
param.help: Default: 0.5

param.label: Maximum secondary alignments to retain
param.help: Default: 50

param.label: Chaining only
param.help: Perform chaining only, without base alignment

param.label: Matching score
param.help: Default: 2

param.label: Mismatch penalty
param.help: Default: 8

param.label: Gap open penalty
param.help: One value, or two comma-separated values (INT1,INT2). Default: 12,23

validator.message: Enter an integer, or two integers separated by a comma (e.g. 12 or 12,23)

param.label: Gap extension penalty
param.help: One value, or two comma-separated values (INT1,INT2). Default: 2,1

validator.message: Enter an integer, or two integers separated by a comma (e.g. 2 or 2,1)

param.label: Minimum DP alignment score
param.help: Suppress alignments with a DP score lower than this value times the matching score. Default: 30

param.name: l_short
param.label: Short-read length threshold
param.help: Treat reads shorter than this as short reads in the default adaptive mode. Default: 325

param.name: base_tag
param.label: Base alignment tag
param.option: Do not output a base alignment tag

param.label: Map Hi-C reads
param.help: Equivalent to taking the smallest-query-position alignment as primary and skipping pairing (-5P)

param.label: Use soft clipping for supplementary alignments

param.name: five
param.label: Take the alignment with the smallest query position as primary
Secondary alignments to output
param.help: Output up to this many secondary alignments. Default: 0

param.label: XA tag threshold
param.help: If there are at most this many hits with a score above 80% of the best hit, output them to the XA tag. Default: 5

tool.help:
**What it does**
`minibwa map` aligns short reads and accurate long reads against a reference genome. It is the successor of `bwa-mem`, combining BWA-MEM variable-length seeding with minimap2-style chaining and base alignment. At comparable accuracy it is several times faster than `bwa-mem` and `bwa-mem2`.
-----
**Reference genome**
You can either select a *built-in* reference genome (indexed transparently for minibwa before mapping) or supply a FASTA file *from your history*. In both cases the wrapper builds the minibwa index automatically before aligning — no separate indexing step is required.
-----
**Read type presets** (``-x``)
- **Mixed short/long reads (adap)** — the default adaptive mode; auto-detects read length. - **Short reads (sr)** — Illumina-style short reads. - **Long reads (lr)** — accurate long reads (e.g. PacBio HiFi).
-----
**Output format**
- **BAM** — a coordinate-sorted, compressed alignment file (SAM converted with samtools). This is the default and the format most downstream Galaxy tools expect. - **PAF** — a compact tabular pairwise mapping format (``-f``), without base-level alignment.
-----
**Read groups**
Adding read group information (``-R``) lets you combine multiple mapping results into a single dataset while keeping track of which reads came from which sample. Many downstream tools (variant callers, duplicate markers) rely on read groups.
-----
**Note**
- Minibwa does not work with noisy long reads or spliced RNA-seq reads. - Minibwa does not support undirectional bisulfite sequencing data. - Minibwa does not recognize alternate haplotypes.
